original_hypothesis.title: Gas Griefing / DoS via _tryGetCredential staticcall to untrusted role providers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: _tryGetCredential uses `staticcall(gas(), ...)` passing all available gas to the external provider. A malicious or buggy pull provider can enter an infinite loop, consuming all gas and causing an Out of Gas revert, effectively DoSing the market's deposit and transfer functions for all users.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Passing all gas to an external contract allows it to grief the transaction. A gas limit (e.g., 50k) should be used for credential checks.
preconditions: Malicious or buggy role provider added by borrower

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unbounded gas passed to external call
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Provider can consume all gas and revert parent tx

analysis_questions: Does this block critical protocol operations?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Provider enters infinite loop, consuming all gas. Lenders cannot deposit or transfer tokens.

validation_result.impact_summary: A buggy or malicious pull provider can consume all gas, causing OOG reverts and DoSing market deposits and transfers for all users.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `_tryGetCredential` function uses inline assembly to perform a `staticcall` to an external role provider, passing `gas()` which forwards all available gas (subject to EIP-150's 63/64 rule). This function is called by `_loopTryGetCredential`, which iterates over all registered pull providers to find a valid credential for a user. If any of the pull providers is buggy or malicious and enters an infinite loop or otherwise consumes all provided gas, it will exhaust 63/64 of the transaction's gas. The parent transaction is left with only 1/64 of the gas, which is typically insufficient to complete the remaining loop iterations or the rest of the transaction logic (such as storage updates in `onDeposit`). This results in an Out of Gas (OOG) revert for the entire transaction. Consequently, a single faulty pull provider can DoS the deposit, transfer, and withdrawal queueing functions for all users who do not already have a valid, unexpired credential.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code explicitly uses `staticcall(gas(), ...)` in `_tryGetCredential` (line 340 in both `FixedTermLoanHooks.sol` and `AccessControlHooks.sol`). There is no gas limit enforced on this external call. Because this call is made within a loop (`_loopTryGetCredential`), the vulnerability is confirmed as a classic gas griefing / DoS vector.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The `staticcall` forwards all available gas (`gas()`) to the external `providerAddress`. A malicious or buggy provider can consume all this gas, leaving the parent transaction with only 1/64 of the gas (per EIP-150), which will likely cause the parent transaction to revert with an Out of Gas error.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When a user without a valid credential calls `onDeposit`, the hook calls `_tryValidateAccessInner`, which falls back to `_loopTryGetCredential`. This function iterates over all `_pullProviders`. For each provider, it calls `_tryGetCredential`, which executes `staticcall(gas(), ...)` to the provider. If the provider consumes all gas, the call OOGs, and the parent transaction is left with 1/64 of the gas, causing the entire `onDeposit` transaction to fail.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A borrower adds a pull provider that later becomes buggy or is upgraded to a malicious implementation that contains an infinite loop. A legitimate lender attempts to deposit into the market. The market calls `onDeposit` on the hooks contract. The hooks contract loops through the pull providers to verify the lender's status. When it calls the buggy provider, the provider consumes all gas. The `staticcall` returns 0, and the parent transaction tries to continue but runs out of its remaining 1/64 gas, reverting the transaction. No new lenders can deposit until the borrower removes the buggy provider.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium. While no funds are directly at risk of theft, the core functionality of the market (deposits, transfers, and potentially queuing withdrawals) is completely denied for users without existing valid credentials. The borrower must intervene to remove the problematic provider to restore functionality.
recommended_mitigations: Impose a strict gas limit on the `staticcall` to the external provider (e.g., `staticcall(50000, providerAddress, ...)`). This ensures that a faulty provider can only consume a bounded amount of gas, allowing the loop to continue and the transaction to succeed.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a mock role provider that contains an infinite loop in its `getCredential` function. 2. Add this provider to the hooks contract using `addRoleProvider`. 3. Attempt to call `onDeposit` as a new lender. 4. Observe the transaction reverting with an Out of Gas error.
